title: crataegus monogyna var. horrida
intro: Crataegus monogyna var. horrida, also known as the thorny hawthorn, is a variety of the hawthorn plant native to Europe and western Asia. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree characterized by its thorny branches and white or pink flowers, which are followed by red berries. The medicinal properties of Crataegus monogyna var. horrida lie in its flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, have been used to promote heart health, reduce blood pressure, and improve overall well-being, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with medications.
